metric_name: OLTP performance improvement over prior generation
notes: HP claims V2200 will provide approximately 50% improvement over current highest OLTP SMP performance.
HyperPlane crossbar operates at 120 MHz over 64-bit path; 15.36 GBytes/sec aggregate bandwidth.
V2200 initial config: up to 16 PA-8200 processors; 16 GB memory; 24 PCI slots.
HP EPS22 (K-Class, 48 processors) scored 3416 QppD vs Sun Ultra Enterprise 10000 at 2009 QppD (64 processors).
Sun UE10000 64-processor TPC-D: 2009 QppD power, 1242 QthD throughput, $3326/QphD price-performance.
IBM RS/6000 SP (128 processors) 1361 QppD vs HP EPS22 (48 processors) 3416 QppD — HP 2.5x better with 62% fewer processors.
Digital 8-processor AlphaServer 350 MHz: over 14,000 tpm OLTP. Vs HP K460 4-way at ~15,000 tpm.
HP announced V2200 for general availability November 1997 with HP-UX 11.0.
HP roadmap: 32-processor V2200 with PA-8500 in 2H1998 delivering 60-90% greater OLTP than initial 1997 model.
HP roadmap: NUMA technology in 1999 V-Class to extend beyond 32-way SMP.
HP anticipated possible Merced integration in 1999 next-gen V-Class.
Aberdeen dismisses NT servers for enterprise production: "trusted for departmental and workgroup applications only." Proved partially incorrect.
HP delivered V2200 approximately on schedule with HP-UX 11.0 in late 1997. HP was known for conservative estimates.
HP 9000 server line discontinued in 2003, superseded by HP Integrity servers (Itanium). Source: Wikipedia HP 9000 article.
Intel Itanium (Merced successor) shipped in 2001, not 1999 as HP suggested. Itanium ultimately failed commercially; discontinued 2021.
Aberdeen's dismissal of NT for enterprise proved wrong. Windows 2000/Server 2003 gained major enterprise production traction by 2001-2003, displacing many UNIX workloads.
Aberdeen noted DEC's irrelevant position in UNIX due to NT focus. Compaq acquired DEC for $9.6B in 1998; Compaq merged with HP in 2002.
HP EPS40 cluster scheduled for late 1997: 16 V2200 nodes, maximum 256 processors for very large data warehouses.
HP WCSO guaranteed 99.95% uptime with financial penalties for exceeding — an early enterprise SLA commitment.
HP offered 100% trade-in credit on T600 or K-Class servers purchased as interim systems — reducing transition risk.
IBM RS/6000 highest OLTP under 7500 tpm vs HP K-Class ~15000 tpm — IBM 2x inferior in OLTP.
